html:
Business Development

Welcome 👋

Welcome to your Business Development Portal. This is your central hub for everything you need to stay connected, organised, and productive as part of the IntelliSOFT Consulting team.

This platform is designed to streamline and simplify various administrative and operational processes, ensuring a more efficient and organized work environment.

Here's what you can manage in Business Development:
Bidding
Manage and track all bidding activities across projects and opportunities.
Bid Preparation
Prepare and organize bid documents, requirements, and submissions efficiently.
Lead Management
Capture, monitor, and nurture leads from initial contact to opportunity stage.
Customer / Client Management
Maintain client records, interactions, and relationships in one place.
Bidding Type
Define and categorize different bidding types for better tracking and reporting.
Opportunity Lost Reasons
Record and analyze reasons for lost opportunities to improve future win rates.
name: Business Development

html:
IntelliSOFT Consulting Limited

Welcome 👋

Welcome to your ICL Staff Portal. This is your central hub for everything you need to stay connected, organised, and productive as part of the IntelliSOFT Consulting team.

This platform is designed to streamline and simplify various administrative and operational processes, ensuring a more efficient and organized work environment.

Here's what you can do on this platform:
HR & People Management
Manage employee records, leave requests, and HR processes seamlessly.
Finance & Expenses
Submit and track expense claims, approvals, and financial requests with ease.
Project & Task Tracking
Stay on top of your deliverables, deadlines, and project milestones.
Internal Communication
Access company announcements, updates, and important notices all in one place.
Reports & Analytics
Generate and view reports to support data-driven decisions across teams.
Compliance & Administration
Handle administrative tasks and ensure organisational compliance effortlessly.
Head over to your ICL Staff workspace on the sidebar to get started
name: Welcome

html:
Lead guide

Lead guide - click to expand

A lead is a potential customer who might be interested in your products or services. Use this guide to create and manage leads effectively in ERPNext.

How to create a lead

  • 1
    Go to the Lead list and click New.
  • 2
    If the person represents an organization, check 'Lead is an Organization'. The Company Name field becomes mandatory - enter it.
  • 3
    If the person is an individual, leave the checkbox unchecked and enter Person Name and Gender.
  • 4
    Enter the Email Address for communication.
  • 5
    Set the Lead Source in the Source field.
  • 6
    Click Save.

Lead status reference

Lead
Default status on creation - action needed.
Open
Sales exec needs to contact the lead.
Replied
Info sent, awaiting lead's response.
Opportunity
Opportunity created - lead is qualified.
Quotation
A quotation has been raised against the lead.
Lost quotation
The quotation given was marked as lost.
Interested
Lead is interested in products or services.
Converted
Sales order confirmed - lead fully converted.
Do not contact
Not interested - no further communication.
i
Status updates automatically based on your actions - creating an Opportunity, raising a Quotation, or confirming a Sales Order. You can also set it manually at any time.
name: Lead Intro

html:
Opportunity guide

Opportunity guide - click to expand

An opportunity is a qualified lead - a potential deal where there is evident interest in a product or service. It can be created from a lead or an existing customer, and multiple opportunities can be collected against one lead or customer.

Key concepts

From a lead
When a lead shows interest in your product or service, convert them directly into an opportunity from the Lead form.
From a customer
You can also create an opportunity against an existing customer - useful for upselling or new product interest.
Multiple opportunities
More than one opportunity can be tracked against a single lead or customer at the same time.

How to create an opportunity

  • 1
    Go to the Opportunity list and click New - or open a Lead and use the Create > Opportunity button.
  • 2
    Set Opportunity From to either Lead or Customer depending on the source.
  • 3
    Select the Lead or Customer name in the Party field.
  • 4
    Set the Opportunity Type - e.g. Sales, Support, or Maintenance.
  • 5
    Enter the Expected Closing Date and set the Probability (%) of winning the deal.
  • 6
    Add the items the prospect is interested in under the Items table.
  • 7
    Click Save. From here you can create a Quotation, Supplier Quotation, or Customer Visit.

Opportunity status reference

Open
Opportunity is active and being pursued.
Replied
Response sent - awaiting further input.
Quotation
A quotation has been raised against this opportunity.
Closed
Opportunity successfully closed and won.
Lost
Opportunity was lost - competitor or no decision.
Cancelled
Opportunity was cancelled before closing.
i
When a lead is converted to an opportunity, the lead status automatically updates to Opportunity - no manual update needed.
✓
Use the Probability (%) field to track pipeline confidence. This feeds into sales forecasting reports in ERPNext automatically.
name: Opportunity Guide
